content.full_text:
NNIA                                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

ITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
, Suite 322
n 95814
322-5660

                                                    August 12, 1975


Mr. Leonard Panish
Registrar-Recorder
County of Los Angeles
30# North Spring
Los Angeles, California 90012

                                    Re: Request for Opinion
                                        No. 75-155

Dear Mr. Panish:

     Thank you for your letter of July 25, 1975, requesting
the Fair Political Practices Commission to issue an opinion
regarding the inclusion of fringe benefits in the salary of
elected officers.

     Your request raises an important question of interpre-
tation of the Political Reform Act and is the subject of
regulations which will be presented to the Commission at a
meeting in October, 1975. We will send you a notice of the
date of the meeting and a copy of the proposed regulation(s)
as soon as they are available.

     Proposed regulation 18620(c) regarding lobbyist employ-
ers provides that routine fringe benefits, such as contributions
to health plans, retirement plans, etc., are not reportable
by the lobbyist and it is anticipated that the draft regula-
tion(s) concerning salaries of elected officers will take the
same approach.

     Although for the reasons stated no formal opinion will
be issued, I hope the foregoing is helpful. If not, you may
request the Commission to review your opinion request at its
September meeting pursuant to 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18321,
a copy of which is enclosed. If you have any questions, I
urge you to contact a member of the Commission staff.

                                    Sincerely,

                                    [signature]
                                    Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                    Chairman

EHL:ugo
Enc.

COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES
DEPARTMENT OF REGISTRAR-RECORDER
605 NORTH SPRING, LOS ANGELES CALIFORNIA 90012

LEONARD PANISH                                      CHARLES L. CAMPBELL, JR.
REG ISTRAR-RECORDER                                 CHIEF DEPUTY

July 23, 1975

Fair Political Practies Commission
926 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

REQUEST FOR OPINION

Gentlemen:

I am requesting an opinion regarding Chapter 4, Article 2, Section
84206.

The final sentence of the section states, in part: "This section is
not applicable to elected officers whose salaries are less than one
hundred dollars ($100) a month..."

Relatively few elected school governing board members in this
county receive a salary which reaches $100 in any month. Several
of the boards or school systems, however, contriute toward health,
hospitalization and/or life insurance plans available to the board
members.

Section 82030, which defines income does not define salary.

With this background, therefore, I ask the following questions:

1.     Does the term "salary" include fringe benefits
       in the form of contributions toward various types
       of insurance plans which directly benefit the
       elected officer and possibly his family?

2.     In determining whether an elected officer has crossed
       the one hundred dollar threshold which creates an
       obligation to file semi-annual campaign statements,
       shall benefits in the form of contributions toward
       insurance plans which directly benefit the individual
       be added to the direct salary?

Very truly yours,

LEONARD PANISH
Registrar-Recorder

FB
